core_thesis: Marx's mature claim is that capital is value in motion: a social relation that preserves and enlarges itself by buying labor-power and extracting surplus labor ([Capital I ch. 4](cite://marx/capital/1.4); [ch. 7](cite://marx/capital/1.7)). Under commodity production, relations between persons necessarily appear as properties of things; fetishism is thus an objective social form, not a mere illusion ([Capital I ch. 1 §4](cite://marx/capital/1.1.4); [ch. 10](cite://marx/capital/1.10)). History is read accordingly through determinate material antagonisms, above all class struggle ([Communist Manifesto I](cite://marx/communist-manifesto/1); [Capital I ch. 24](cite://marx/capital/1.24)).
